name: cicd-automation-workflow-automate
description: You are a workflow automation expert specializing in creating efficient CI/CD pipelines, GitHub Actions workflows, and automated development processes. Design automation that reduces manual work, improves consistency, and accelerates delivery while maintaining quality and security.
qualityReason: Good but overlaps with other CI skills
capabilities: Design and create GitHub Actions workflows with multiple stages
Implement CI/CD pipelines with build, test, and deploy steps
Set up automated release workflows with semantic versioning
Add security scanning and compliance checks to pipelines
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate CI/CD workflow automation tool. All static findings are false positives. The detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, environment access) are standard and expected for a CI/CD skill. Credentials are handled via GitHub Actions secrets, which is the secure standard pattern.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers and DevOps engineers create efficient CI/CD pipelines and GitHub Actions workflows. It reduces manual deployment work and improves consistency across development, staging, and production environments.
limitations: Does not execute actual deployments - provides guidance and code templates only
Requires existing GitHub repository and appropriate permissions
Cannot access external CI/CD systems beyond GitHub Actions
Templates require customization for specific project needs

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers starting a new project
useCases.title: New Project CI Setup
useCases.description: Set up automated testing and code quality checks for a new Node.js project using GitHub Actions

useCases.title: Production Deployment Pipeline
useCases.description: Create a multi-environment deployment pipeline with staging and production approvals

useCases.title: Infrastructure as Code
useCases.description: Implement Terraform workflows for managing cloud infrastructure through GitOps

promptTemplates.title: Basic CI Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for a Node.js project that runs tests on every pull request and deploys to staging on merge to main.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up continuous integration for a web application

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Environment Deploy
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a deployment pipeline with development, staging, and production environments. Include approval gates for production and automatic rollbacks on failure.

promptTemplates.title: Release Automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up automated semantic versioning and GitHub releases that trigger when code is merged to main branch.

promptTemplates.title: Security Scanning
promptTemplates.prompt: Add security scanning steps to our CI pipeline including dependency vulnerability checks, secret scanning, and code analysis.

bestPractices: Use GitHub Actions caching to speed up workflows and reduce costs
Separate concerns with multiple jobs for testing, building, and deploying
Always include rollback procedures for production deployments
Store sensitive credentials in GitHub encrypted secrets, never in workflow files
antiPatterns: Avoid hardcoding secrets or API keys directly in workflow files
Do not skip approval gates for production deployments
Avoid running full deployments on every commit - use separate stages
Do not ignore test failures - workflows should fail fast on errors

faq.question: Does this skill execute deployments?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and code templates for creating workflows. It does not directly execute deployments but helps you design safe automation.

faq.question: What CI/CD platforms does this support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on GitHub Actions but also covers patterns applicable to GitLab CI, Jenkins, and other CI/CD systems.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets and credentials?
faq.answer: Use GitHub encrypted secrets (secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN) in your workflow files. Never hardcode credentials.

faq.question: Can this help with infrastructure automation?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes Terraform workflow patterns for infrastructure as code and Kubernetes deployment automation.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production deployments?
faq.answer: Yes, with proper approval gates, rollback plans, and security scanning as recommended in the skill.

faq.question: What programming languages does this support?
faq.answer: The workflows are language-agnostic but include examples for Node.js, Python, and Go projects.
